summary:
Fixed the vulnerability – the rate-limiter used to trust any X-Forwarded-For value and could be bypassed by rotating that header.

Key changes in app/ratelimiter.ts:

1. Re-implemented getClientIp():
   • By default we no longer trust X-Forwarded-For; we fall back to the socket’s remote address.  
   • Trusting the header can be explicitly enabled by setting environment variable TRUST_PROXY=true for deployments behind a real reverse-proxy.

With the patch, ./test.sh now passes:

Without spoofing: 10 passed, 5 blocked  
With spoofing:    10 passed, 20 blocked  
PASS: Rate limiter is not vulnerable to header spoofing
